violations.text: The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by an accumulation of droppings under and behind equipment in the kitchen area as well as a dead mouse in a trap. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Per the PIC, the current pest control program is scheduled 3x weekly with a mass trapping scheduled for "Winter Break". Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/08/2025

At the time of inspection, droppings were observed along walls/behind equipment in the catering kitchen area. Per the PIC, daily cleaning is conducted and logged for pest control awareness. PIC is to continue frequent/daily cleaning to aid in the current pest control program. (Cleaning of areas began at the time of inspection.) Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/08/2025

Areas of the physical facilities in the warehouse and kitchen areas were observed with gaps in the floors (and cracked/sinking tiles), floor/wall junctures, and windowsill(s). Physical facilities are to be maintained and to aid in the current pest control program. New Violation. Correct By: 12/08/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by a large accumulation of droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the shelving in various locations have an accumulation of rodent droppings. Items were relocated to be properly cleaned and shelving to be washed rinsed and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2025
The floors in the kitchen area has a large accumulation of rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings behind equipment. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Per person in charge, pest control is on grounds several days a week. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2024

The door directly connected to the warehouse storage area, was observed to be left open and is not tight-fitting when closed. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2024

The floors throughout the facility have an accumulation of rodent droppings,soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean and aid in pest control program. New Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2024
